reviews.text:
Visited again today, flat white 550. More sitting now. Changed review from 3 star to 4.

Best domingo loop real breakfast options we find. And very good flat white 380. Breakfast 800pesos come with two toast. And they have real flat white, 380 pesos. Price in July 2022. But their bakery was terrible. We ordered chocolate roll and croissant 350 pesos each, Small and cold and not fresh very sad at cafe side, we should buy one next door the bakery side.but why they serve cold one? :(

A year ago I left a rather critical review here, and I feel it is only fair to update it now. The change has been remarkable. The service has improved significantly — the staff are friendly, polite, fast, and attentive. Everything is handled with care and professionalism.

The café itself has also been redesigned, and the new interior looks fantastic — modern, stylish, and very pleasant to spend time in. Most importantly, the coffee is once again excellent, just as it used to be at its best.

It is great to see a place that actually listens to feedback and takes it seriously. I am happy to come back and can confidently recommend it again.

A year ago:

They are used to be the best place for coffee in BA. The coffee was special and delicious. Now, during last month, I constantly observe that they take money for two caps (my choice) and give only one, before I ask them again. Cappuccino is always cold. Instead of double espresso you get an americano. The taste of coffee is not as before. There is no need to visit this place anymore.

When looking for a great coffee in town one should start here, once you drink this all day need is satisfied. Perfect barista style well roasted with deep flavor… the place has a nice streetside sitting area, parking for bicycles and a bench under the window to wait for your takeaway. Real city style coffee roasters that all your expectations are met. Almond milk is fresh and makes great foam for strong aromatic flat white. Croissant is better at Paul yet the surface was crunchy, buttery and salty as we dreamed of, interior bit bread like, but delicious.

One of the best espresso (and coffee in general) in Buenos Aires. It might not be for everyone, though, as in true speciality coffee way, the espresso has a good dose of acidity and character (it is not your average bland coffee). Espresso is prepared in the traditional Italian way (size) so it might seem very small to anyone who hasn't been to Italy, but it comes with a perfect, delicious, hazelnut color crema. Too bad they do not serve a small glass of water with the espresso, otherwise it would feel like being in Italy. The flat white is also very nice, although it comes with a bit too much milk (like a cappuccino with a double shot of espresso). The cafe is on the small side, with two long "communal" tables which are fun to share with other customers and some smaller tables and chairs on the sidewalk in front, under some magnificent big trees. Highly recommended!

For a good cappuccino and nice environment visit here.  I didn't have their coffee but I did have the lattes and cappuccinos, both tasty and pretty.  If they get WiFi my review goes from 4 to 5 stars.
